currency.baseNote: Rates show ZMW per 1 unit of foreign currency. ↓ ZMW = Kwacha strengthening (appreciating).
currency.mpRateNote: Monetary Policy Rate held at 13.50% — reduced 75bps from 14.25% on Feb 11, 2026. BOZ held rate at May 2026 review. Next review: August 2026.

USD.note: Jul 9: 1 USD ≈ 18.1485 ZMW mid-market. Kwacha strengthened sharply from 18.4668 (Jul 8) — best level in weeks. Market trending lower in 18.00–18.50 range.

GBP.note: 1 GBP ≈ 24.1571 ZMW (Jul 9). Sterling slightly softer from 24.27 (Jul 7). Kwacha strengthening against GBP. Down ~0.5% day-on-day.

EUR.note: 1 EUR ≈ 20.7637 ZMW (Jul 9). Euro notably weaker from 21.09 (Jul 7). Kwacha gaining ground against single currency.

ZAR.note: 1 ZAR ≈ 1.1049 ZMW (Jul 9). Rand broadly stable. 1 ZMW ≈ 0.905 ZAR.

BWP.note: Botswana pula. 1 BWP ≈ 1.283 ZMW. Stable cross-rate.

CNY.note: Chinese yuan. 1 CNY ≈ 2.69 ZMW. Cross-rate stable.

trendAnalysis.direction: Kwacha strengthened sharply — mid-market at 18.1485 (Jul 9) vs 18.4668 (Jul 8). Best level in weeks. Kwacha notably firmer across major crosses — USD, GBP, EUR all lower. EM currencies mixed.
drivers: Record copper output 890K tonnes (2025) — strong export earnings
Record maize harvest >4M tonnes — reduced food import needs
Debt restructuring ~94% complete — sovereign risk premium collapsing
IMF successor programme talks scheduled July 2026 — staff visit confirmed
MPR at 13.5% — high real yields attracting carry trade inflows
Yuan mining tax corridor reducing USD demand
Gross international reserves at historic high of US$6.5 billion
ERB cut fuel prices for July — lower import bill supporting kwacha
Pre-election spending starting to add pressure on kwacha
trendAnalysis.outlook: Kwacha consolidating in 18.00–18.50 range, with strong gains seen on Jul 9. BOZ likely to hold MPR at August review. Pre-election spending adding modest pressure. Fuel price cut provides offset.
trendAnalysis.projection3m: USD/ZMW 16.50–18.50 range. Elections expected to drive volatility.
trendAnalysis.projection6m: USD/ZMW 16.00–19.00 range. Post-election clarity determines direction.
